spec: RFC-0008 §3.3 (L2 PoUH committee aggregate signatures); IETF draft-irtf-cfrg-bls-signature-05 (BLS Signatures) with the BLS_SIG_BLS12381G2_XMD:SHA-256_SSWU_RO_NUL_ ciphersuite
vendor: supranational/blst v0.3.15 portable C path (https://github.com/supranational/blst/releases/tag/v0.3.15); ciphersuite identifier matches the IETF draft 'Basic' scheme with the minimal-pubkey-size variant (PK in G1 = 48 bytes compressed, signature in G2 = 96 bytes compressed)
notes: All vectors below use small fixed integer secret keys (sk = 1, 2, 3) for reproducibility; ANY conformant BLS implementation MUST produce byte-for-byte identical pk/sig values. The first vector (sk=1) is a universal cross-implementation invariant: pk(1) is the G1 generator's compressed Zcash encoding.

aggregate_vectors.name: aggregate(sig1, sig2, sig3) over the same message; verify against (pk1, pk2, pk3)
aggregate_vectors.notes: FastAggregateVerify per draft-irtf-cfrg-bls-signature-05 §3.3.4: all n public keys MUST correspond to signatures over the SAME message. Used by the L2 PoUH committee to finalise blocks with a single 96-byte signature regardless of committee size.

negative_vectors.name: scalar 0 is not a valid SK
negative_vectors.notes: blst_sk_check rejects scalar 0 and scalars ≥ curve order; the wrapper surfaces this as MALFORMED.

negative_vectors.name: tampered signature (bit-flip in sig[0])
negative_vectors.notes: Single-bit flip either yields a non-G2 decompression or a wrong G2 point; both reject.

negative_vectors.name: cross-verification (sig1 against pk2)
negative_vectors.notes: Aspect of unforgeability: sig produced by SK_a does not verify under PK_b.

negative_vectors.name: wrong-message aggregate-verify
negative_vectors.based_on: aggregate(sig1,sig2,sig3) over message A, verify_aggregate over message B
negative_vectors.notes: FastAggregateVerify is binding to the message; verifying against a different message MUST fail.
